content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 21, 2010
Fred Strong
PO Box 3621
Paso Robles, CA 93447

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our file No. W-10-169

Dear Mr. Strong:

This letter responds to your request for advice, on behalf of yourself regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); Regulation 18329(a), copy enclosed.)

Based on our telephone conversation on October 19, 2010 and a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide advice at this time. Your current question involves an analysis of actions already taken. Issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by Section 83111.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division

HMR:jgl

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked for advice regarding campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act, but the specific nature of the question is not detailed in this declination letter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the request involved analysis of past conduct rather than prospective guidance, which would not serve the impartial administration of the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is a declination letter without a separate QUESTION section; the question content is inferred from the letter body. The conclusion is clearly articulated in the second paragraph. OCR quality is generally good with minor artifacts.
